QF.id: XIII Introduction to the Underlying Markets_41

question.text: If USDJPY is quoted at 115.08/115.09 and USDINR at 75.64/75.65, what is the offer rate for JPYINR (price of 100 JPY in INR)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** To buy 100 JPY with INR (offer): Buy USD with INR at USDINR offer (75.65), then sell USD for JPY at USDJPY bid (115.08 — bank buys USD at bid, giving you JPY). JPYINR offer = (75.65/115.08) × 100 = 65.7369 ≈ 65.74. Note: we use USDJPY bid because we want to SELL USD for JPY, and the bank buys at bid. **The Trap:** When USD is base currency in both pairs, we divide (not multiply). Using the wrong USDJPY rate (bid vs offer) is the common error. The bid side: 75.64/115.09 × 100 = 65.72. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series I Ch1 covers JPYINR cross-rate calculation. This involves division unlike EURINR/GBPINR which use multiplication. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** JPYINR cross-rate calculation is trickier because USD is base in both underlying pairs — always identify which side (bid/offer) corresponds to your buy/sell intent.
